paper_title: Intramolecular 13C analysis of tree rings provides multiple plant ecophysiology signals covering decades

methods.description: Calculation of Pearson correlation coefficients to assess the linear relationship between different variables, including isotopic signals (Δ, Δ i ′) and environmental variables (VPD), as well as relationships between different intramolecular isotopic positions (Δ i ′).

steps.description: Calculate correlation coefficient for the Δ~VPD relationship.

steps.description: Calculate correlation coefficient for the Δ 1 ′~VPD relationship.

steps.description: Calculate correlation coefficient for the Δ 1 ′ and Δ 2 ′ relationship.

steps.description: Calculate correlation coefficient for the Δ 5 ′ and Δ 6 ′ relationship.

steps.description: Calculate correlation coefficient for the Δ 1 ′ and Δ 6 ′ relationship.

steps.description: Calculate correlation coefficient for the Δ 2 ′ and Δ 5 ′ relationship.

methods.description: Building simple linear regression models to investigate the relationship between a single predictor variable (Δ or Δ 1 ′) and the response variable (VPD).

steps.description: Build a simple linear regression model with VPD as the response and Δ as the predictor (VPD~Δ).
Build a simple linear regression model with VPD as the response and Δ 1 ′ as the predictor (VPD~Δ 1 ′).

methods.method_name: Multiple Linear Regression Modeling with Automatic Model Selection
methods.description: Building a multiple linear regression model to predict VPD using a combination of intramolecular isotopic signals (Δ i ′ components), employing automatic model selection to identify the best set of predictors.

steps.description: Combine multiple linear regression modeling with automatic model selection techniques.
steps.searchable_summary: perform multiple linear regression with automatic model selection
outputs: Selected multiple linear regression model (e.g., VPD~Δ 1 ′ + Δ 3 ′ + Δ 5 ′)

methods.method_name: Regression Model Evaluation (R-squared and Adjusted R-squared)
methods.description: Evaluating the goodness-of-fit of regression models using R-squared and adjusted R-squared, which accounts for the number of predictors, enabling comparison between models with different complexities.

steps.description: Calculate adjusted R-squared (adjR²) for the simple linear regression model VPD~Δ.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate adjusted R-squared for a regression model

steps.description: Calculate adjusted R-squared (adjR²) for the multiple linear regression model VPD~Δ 1 ′ + Δ 3 ′ + Δ 5 ′.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate adjusted R-squared for a regression model
inputs: Multiple linear regression model (VPD~Δ 1 ′ + Δ 3 ′ + Δ 5 ′)
outputs: Adjusted R-squared value for VPD~Δ 1 ′ + Δ 3 ′ + Δ 5 ′

steps.description: Compare the models based on their adjusted R-squared values.
steps.searchable_summary: compare regression models based on adjusted R-squared
inputs: Adjusted R-squared value for VPD~Δ 1 ′ + Δ 3 ′ + Δ 5 ′

methods.description: Testing the predictive performance of regression models using 10-fold cross-validation to obtain a more robust estimate of model performance on unseen data.

steps.description: Test the predictive abilities of the Δ i ′ model (VPD~Δ 1 ′ + Δ 3 ′ + Δ 5 ′) by 10-fold cross-validation.
steps.searchable_summary: perform 10-fold cross-validation on a regression model
inputs: Multiple linear regression model (VPD~Δ 1 ′ + Δ 3 ′ + Δ 5 ′)
outputs: Cross-validated R-squared (Q²) for VPD~Δ 1 ′ + Δ 3 ′ + Δ 5 ′

steps.description: Test the predictive abilities of the Δ model (VPD~Δ) by 10-fold cross-validation.
steps.searchable_summary: perform 10-fold cross-validation on a regression model

steps.description: Compare the predictive abilities of both models based on their Q² values.
steps.searchable_summary: compare regression models based on cross-validated R-squared (Q-squared)
inputs: Cross-validated R-squared (Q²) for VPD~Δ 1 ′ + Δ 3 ′ + Δ 5 ′

methods.method_name: Hierarchical Cluster Analysis of Intramolecular Isotopic Signals
methods.description: Applying hierarchical cluster analysis to the intramolecular isotopic signals (Δ i ′) to identify groups of signals that exhibit similar variability patterns, suggesting position-specific fractionation effects.

steps.description: Screen for position-specific signals by hierarchical cluster analysis of Δ i ′.
steps.searchable_summary: perform hierarchical cluster analysis on time series data
outputs: Identified clusters of Δ i ′ positions
